steps.title: Track the result
steps.content: The first sentence says the graphics come from perfect geometric shapes and therefore lack organic realism. The second sentence gives the consequence: the 3D elements may have unnaturally precise angles and curves. The transition must signal that the second idea follows from the first.

steps.title: Reject unrelated links
steps.content: Choice B adds another point instead of showing a consequence. Choice C signals a broad conclusion, but the second sentence is a specific result. Choice D signals contrast, while the precise angles directly follow from the perfect geometric shapes. The needed transition marks a logical result. The answer is A.
